content.text:
[cron:33ed09c3-35da-4b89-933e-9805fff6119e Email Colosseum Tournament] Run 50 Email Colosseum battles: cd ~/.openclaw/workspace/tools && source .venv/bin/activate && cd ~/.openclaw/workspace/colosseum/email_ad_domain && python3 -c "
from battle_engine import run_battle, get_db, get_top_performers
import random
conn = get_db()
cursor = conn.cursor()
cursor.execute('SELECT id FROM beings WHERE type = \"subject_line\"')
ids = [r[0] for r in cursor.fetchall()]
conn.close()
for i in range(50):
    a, b = random.sample(ids, 2)
    try: run_battle(a, b, battle_type='subject_line')
    except: pass
top = get_top_performers('subject_line', 5)
for t in top:
    print(f'#{t[\"id\"]} Score:{t[\"score\"]:.2f} W:{t[\"wins\"]} L:{t[\"losses\"]} | {t[\"content\"][:50]}')
". Report the top 5 champions.
Current time: Saturday, March 7th, 2026 — 2:02 AM (America/New_York)

Return your summary as plain text; it will be delivered automatically. If the task explicitly calls for messaging a specific external recipient, note who/where it should go instead of sending it yourself.

cd ~/.openclaw/workspace/tools && source .venv/bin/activate && cd ~/.openclaw/workspace/colosseum/email_ad_domain && python3 -c "
from battle_engine import run_battle, get_db, get_top_performers
import random
conn = get_db()
cursor = conn.cursor()
cursor.execute('SELECT id FROM beings WHERE type = \"subject_line\"')
ids = [r[0] for r in cursor.fetchall()]
conn.close()
for i in range(50):
    a, b = random.sample(ids, 2)
    try: run_battle(a, b, battle_type='subject_line')
    except: pass
top = get_top_performers('subject_line', 5)
for t in top:
    print(f'#{t[\"id\"]} Score:{t[\"score\"]:.2f} W:{t[\"wins\"]} L:{t[\"losses\"]} | {t[\"content\"][:50]}')
"

content.text:
50 battles complete. The Colosseum keeps sharpening the steel.

**🏆 Top 5 Email Subject Line Champions:**

| Rank | ID | Score | W-L | Subject Line |
|------|-----|-------|------|-------------|
| 1 | #4 | 48.20 | 473-82 | *I reviewed 500 PI cases. Here's what the winners h…* |
| 2 | #1 | 46.80 | 478-120 | *The 3-second mistake costing PI attorneys $47K per…* |
| 3 | #22 | 46.60 | 448-64 | *3-second fix to stop being the $47K bottleneck* |
| 4 | #21 | 45.70 | 430-46 | *Avoid the $47K oversight slowing your practice* |
| 5 | #30 | 44.55 | 426-61 | *The $47K oversight: Are you the bottleneck?* |

**#4** holds the throne — the "I reviewed 500 PI cases" opener with a dominant 85.2% win rate. That social proof + curiosity combo keeps winning. **#21** has the best win rate of the bunch at 90.3% (430-46) but lower total score from fewer battles — one to watch as it gets more reps. The $47K figure and bottleneck framing clearly resonate across the board.
